structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages OpenGL shaders. It uses the `GL20` package for OpenGL operations. The code loads shader source code from files using the `getText()` method, compiles and links the shaders to create a program, and validates the program's linking status. It also provides methods for binding the program and uniform values.

items.description: in Java is a class that provides functionality for loading and managing shaders, including uniform and matrix4f functions. It also includes loadShader and validateProgram methods to load and validate the shaders. Additionally, it has private fields and methods to handle the GL20 API calls.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to the current GL context, enabling its use for rendering and other graphics operations.
items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `program`. The returned value is an integer.

returns.description: an integer representing the value of the `program` field.

usage.code:
Shader shader = Shader.load("shader");
int program = shader.getProgram();
usage.description: This code loads a shader from the file "shader", and then stores the value returned by the getProgram() method in the variable "program". The value stored in "program" can be used to interact with the shader, such as binding it or setting its uniform variables.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: in Java takes a string name and an array of floats as input, and uses the GL20 class to set uniform values for a shader program based on the length of the input array.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location within the current program.

usage.code:
Shader shader = new Shader("shader.vert"); // Loads the vertex and fragment shaders from "shader.vert" file
shader.bind(); // Binds the loaded shader to the current GL context
shader.uniformf("myUniform", 1.0f, 2.0f, 3.0f); // Sets the value of a uniform variable in the shader named "myUniform" with values (1.0, 2.0, and 3.0)

items.name: uniformf

items.description: glues a uniform matrix to the GPU. It takes a name and a Matrix4f object as input, then passes the matrix data to GL20.glUniformMatrix4 with the appropriate location and transpose parameters.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform to be updated with the matrix value.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 matrix that will be stored and accessed as a uniform buffer in the shader program.

* The `Matrix4f` class represents a 4D matrix with floating-point elements.
* The `getLinearData()` method returns a copy of the matrix's linear data as an array of floats.
* The `Util.createFlippedBuffer()` method creates a new buffer that stores the mirrored version of the input matrix.

usage.description: In this example, the method `uniformMat4` is called with the parameter "model" and a Matrix4f object as its value. The `uniformMat4` method would then use the getLoaction method to retrieve the location of the uniform variable in the shader program and call the GL20.glUniformMatrix4 method with the appropriate parameters to set the uniform variable to the value of the matrix object.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform variable in a GPU program specified by the `name` parameter using the `GL20.glGetUniformLocation()` method.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of an uniform location within the currently active program for which to retrieve information, as passed through the `GL20.glGetUniformLocation()` method.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform variable in a graphics processing unit (GPU) program.

usage.description: In the above code, the program variable refers to a shader program that has been created and linked using the loadShader method provided in this class. The getLoaction method is then used to retrieve a location for a specific uniform variable within the shader program by passing in the name of the variable as a string argument. This location can then be used to set values for the uniform variable using other methods such as GL20.glUniform1f() or GL20.glUniformMatrix4().

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader program from a file and validates it for use in a graphics application.

params.name: filename
params.description: name of the shader file to be loaded.

returns.description:
a `Shader` object representing the combined vertex and fragment shaders loaded from a file.

* The returned value is an instance of `Shader`, which represents a shader program that has been loaded from a file.
* The `Shader` object contains information about the shader program, such as its name and the names of its vertex and fragment shaders.
* The `program` field of the `Shader` object refers to the OpenGL program handle created by `glCreateProgram()`.
* The `validateProgram()` function is called after the shader programs are loaded to ensure that they are valid and can be used for rendering.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a shader program and compiles it from a string source, then attaches it to a program handle.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader being created, which determines the shader's behavior and functionality.

params.name: src
params.description: source code of the shader to be compiled.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program that the loaded shader will be attached to.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program by checking its linking and validation status, printing any error messages if they occur.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D programming handle that is being validated and linked.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a specified file as a string and returns it.

params.name: file
params.description: file from which the text is to be read.

returns.description: a string representation of the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText
